title: Murder Mystery

jokes.text: Okay, before we start, I just wanna warn everybody, there is a murderer on the loose. In this town. - So heads up, eyes peeled, guys.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn delivers serious warning about a murderer to choir group
jokes.punchline: Revealed to be about Twyla's murder mystery party
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection joke with good build-up and payoff, well-structured opener

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: I need to warn you all about something very serious. There's a murderer in our community, and we need to be extremely careful.

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: It's for my murder mystery party this weekend!

jokes.text: John and I used to attend "Eyes Wide Shut" parties at the Castros'. Though... I'm guessing your evening's activities might be somewhat different.
jokes.setup: Moira compares murder mystery party to their past social events
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions attending Eyes Wide Shut-style parties
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira character moment - casually referencing something scandalous while maintaining her sophisticated demeanor

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, a murder mystery party is hardly our first foray into elaborate social events. We've attended far more... exclusive gatherings. Why, I once went to a party where everyone wore masks and very little else. It was very Eyes Wide Shut.

jokes.text: Oh well, the party's actually Friday. I had a 50/50 chance there.
jokes.setup: Moira says they have plans Saturday night
jokes.punchline: Twyla admits she just guessed the day wrong
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven humor showing Twyla's honest, unpretentious nature

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: We have plans Saturday night.

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Oh. I just guessed the day wrong.

jokes.text: Oh, I would be pleased to RSVP as... pending. Just uh, as soon as I double check, and circle back.
jokes.setup: Moira being asked to commit to the party
jokes.punchline: Uses overly formal business language to avoid committing
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira - using unnecessarily formal language in casual situations

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I appreciate the invitation, but I find myself in a position where I must respectfully table this matter pending a comprehensive review of my scheduling infrastructure and a full risk assessment of the event parameters.

jokes.text: A weekend of golf, and a stay at a luxurious motel. What motel is that? I'm gonna let them work out the verbiage
jokes.setup: Johnny pitching luxurious motel package
jokes.punchline: Stevie's deadpan question about which luxurious motel they're referring to
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic - Johnny's optimism vs. Stevie's realism about their motel

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: We're going to position the Rosebud as a luxury destination. We'll offer premium amenities, impeccable service, an elevated experience.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Which luxury motel are we talking about?

jokes.text: Except there was this one point in high school where I got this haircut that everyone said made me look like I was in the LPGA.
jokes.setup: Johnny suggesting golf isn't Stevie's thing
jokes.punchline: Stevie's deadpan reference to looking like a professional female golfer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie delivery - unexpected personal detail delivered deadpan

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Well, I mean, I do look like a professional female golfer.

jokes.text: I might even let you drive the cart. - Can you drink on the cart? - Yeah. Well, I will get the "back in 15 minutes" sign.
jokes.setup: Johnny offering to let Stevie drive golf cart as incentive
jokes.punchline: Stevie only cares if she can drink, immediately agrees when told yes
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Stevie's priorities and practical approach

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: You can drive the golf cart.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Can I drink?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Okay, I'm in.

jokes.text: Well, it's gonna take longer than 15 minutes. You have to give people hope.
jokes.setup: Stevie getting the 15-minute sign for their golf trip
jokes.punchline: Stevie's cynical but practical business philosophy
jokes.explanation: Perfectly encapsulates the show's blend of cynicism and hope

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: You know, I've learned that in business, you have to be willing to do things that make you uncomfortable. Like, really uncomfortable. Like, gag reflex uncomfortable.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: That's... not the philosophy I was hoping to hear.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Well, that's the one you're getting. Because at the end of the day, money doesn't care about your feelings, Johnny. It only cares about your willingness to debase yourself.

jokes.text: Understandably, yes, BLT please, no bread.
jokes.setup: Twyla asking if Moira is coming to party
jokes.punchline: Moira misunderstands, orders a breadless BLT
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira absurdity - ordering a sandwich that's essentially just lettuce and tomato

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Are you coming to the party?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: A party? Oh, I'd love to, but I'm famished. Could you have them prepare me a BLT?

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: No, I meant—

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: And hold the bread. I'm watching my carbs.

jokes.text: I was actually thinking bagel bites. - Ah! Did I mention it was a potluck? Oh, you most certainly did not.
jokes.setup: Twyla hoping Moira will bring something special to party
jokes.punchline: Moira's indignant reaction to learning it's potluck
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing class differences and expectations

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: I was really hoping you'd bring something special to the party.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Of course I'm bringing something special. I'm Moira Rose.

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: It's potluck.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: A potluck? Twyla, I don't do potluck. I do pot roast. I do pot pie. I do not do potluck.

jokes.text: No one's coming to your party? Well, in fairness, the party is still a few hours away.
jokes.setup: Moira horrified that no one is coming
jokes.punchline: Twyla's optimistic but sad rationalization
jokes.explanation: Sweet cringe comedy showing Twyla's eternal optimism despite disappointment

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: No one is coming.

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Well, maybe they're all just busy. Or maybe they don't know where the venue is. Or maybe they think it's next weekend. Or maybe they all died.

jokes.text: And some people did say that if another thing that's supposed to happen doesn't happen, then they might be able to come, so, that's good.
jokes.setup: Twyla trying to sound optimistic about attendance
jokes.punchline: Convoluted explanation of conditional maybe-attendees
jokes.explanation: Painfully realistic portrayal of trying to sound positive about bad social situation

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Well, I mean, if Brenda's cousin's friend can get off work, and if the weather is nice, and if my car doesn't break down again, then maybe we'll have a really good turnout.

jokes.text: Well, I shouldn't have said no one, My mom will come. And she'll probably bring whoever she's dating. And he'll most likely bring some younger friend.
jokes.setup: Twyla correcting herself about having no guests
jokes.punchline: Pathetic guest list that's basically her mom's dating situation
jokes.explanation: Nicely escalating reveal of how sad her party situation really is

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: Well, I do have some guests coming. I have a pathetic guest list that's basically my mom's dating situation.

jokes.text: You'd be shocked at how many celebrities show up to a thing with the promise of a free Wagyu slider. I-I'm not charging anyone, this is just for fun. Oh, this is worse than I assumed!
jokes.setup: Moira giving party advice about comping influential guests
jokes.punchline: Learning it's free makes Moira even more horrified
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Moira's complete misunderstanding of small-town social dynamics

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: You cannot comp influential guests. It sends the wrong message.

dialogue.speaker: Twyla
dialogue.line: But Moira, it's free anyway. We're not losing money.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Free?! Oh my God, that's even worse. Now they think we're desperate.

jokes.text: Did you know you can still get mail? Yes, I know about mail. No, but I'm talking about like, mail, mail. Like a little man in a uniform, with his satchel full of letters, traveling door to door.
jokes.setup: Alexis discovering mail delivery
jokes.punchline: Describing mailman like it's a fascinating anthropological discovery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis moment - treating basic services like exotic discoveries due to privileged background

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, David, there's a man. He just... he comes to the house. Every day. With letters.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, that's the mailman.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: But why? Why does he do that? Is he being held captive? Does he need help?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: No, Alexis. It's his job.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: His job is to come into our house uninvited and leave things? That's either a job or a crime, David, and I'm not sure which.

jokes.text: Um, that's a mailman. And he comes to your house, you pay him, and he gives you your stuff. I think it's free delivery, David. I don't think so...
jokes.setup: David explaining mailman to Alexis
jokes.punchline: David doesn't believe mail delivery is free
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic showing both are hilariously out of touch with normal services

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Wait, so he just... comes to our house every day?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: And he brings us things?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: For free?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, no. I'm sure there's a fee involved somewhere. No one does anything for free.

jokes.text: And he comes to your house, you pay him, and he gives you your stuff. I think it's free delivery, David. I don't think so...
jokes.setup: Alexis thinks you pay the mailman directly for delivery
jokes.punchline: David corrects her but then doubts himself about basic postal service
jokes.explanation: Shows how out of touch both Rose children are with normal life.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: So like, how much do you usually tip the mailman? Because I've been doing like fifty bucks a week.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Alexis, you don't tip the mailman. He's a federal employee. The post office is funded by taxes.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, so I've just been giving money to a stranger?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes. Although, now that I'm saying it out loud, I'm not entirely sure how the postal service works.

jokes.text: it's just that the town only has so many ex-boyfriends to hand out secretary work. Um, first of all, David, I'm an assistant, not a secretary, And second of all, Ted is not my ex-boyfriend, he is ex-fiance.
jokes.setup: David commenting on Alexis hanging around
jokes.punchline: Alexis correcting both job title and relationship status, making it worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation where Alexis's corrections actually make her situation sound more pathetic

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You're always hanging around here. Don't you have a job?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I'm not hanging around, David. I'm the PR consultant for the Woebegone Clothing Company.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That closed down three months ago.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, I'm also dating Ted now, so I have a lot going on.

jokes.text: You've circled "Stars and Cosmology?" Oh yeah, I thought it said "Cosmetology."
jokes.setup: David questioning Alexis's course selection
jokes.punchline: Alexis confused astronomy with beauty school
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Alexis's beauty-focused worldview leading to simple mistake

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You're taking astronomy?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Yeah, I thought it would be like, astrology, you know? Like reading people's signs and stuff.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's astrology, not astronomy.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, I didn't know that! I thought it was like a really exclusive thing, like, you know, only beautiful people could do it.

jokes.text: Well, the set up is very important, Stevie. Uh, legs so far apart, - knees slightly bent. - Ooh! Make sure you're nice and balanced over the ball. Yeah, woo!
jokes.setup: Roland demonstrating golf stance on Johnny
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo in golf instruction with Stevie's reactions
jokes.explanation: Relies on cheap sexual innuendo rather than character-driven humor

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Now, you want to grip it firmly, but not too tight. You gotta feel it in your hands.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Okay.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: And then you're gonna want to spread your legs a little wider for better balance.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Right.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Now bend over and really get your rear end up in the air.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I'm not doing that.

jokes.text: Right, okay, so just first off, spread your legs just a little wider, if you would, for me. There, see, Stevie? They've gotta be like, sort of even with the shoulders, and if you would just pop the old tush... Just go, Bam!
jokes.setup: Roland continuing golf instruction
jokes.punchline: Increasingly inappropriate physical positioning
jokes.explanation: Continues cheap physical comedy without much wit or character development

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Okay, now spread your legs a little wider. No, wider. Come on, don't be shy.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Roland, this is getting very uncomfortable.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Just trust me. Now bend over and grab your ankles.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'm not doing that.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Johnny, I'm trying to teach you golf here. Now turn around and let me get behind you to show you the proper grip.

jokes.text: Are you using the Vargon grip? I'm using La Va Grande.
jokes.setup: Johnny and Roland discussing golf grips
jokes.punchline: Made-up fancy golf grip names
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing both men trying to sound knowledgeable

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: These golf grips are ridiculously expensive. What makes them worth the price?

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, they've got names like the Velvet Hammer, the Sultan's Caress, the Gentleman's Touch...

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Those aren't real grip names, are they?

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: No, but they sound expensive, don't they?

jokes.text: Yeah, this is just a little hard to understand, so I want a record of it. Okay, delete that, please! Can I get a copy of that?
jokes.setup: Stevie filming the awkward golf lesson
jokes.punchline: Johnny's panic vs Roland's enthusiasm about being filmed
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the physical comedy setup with character-appropriate reactions

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Are we... are we rolling right now?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Yeah, we're rolling.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Oh God. Um, okay. Just so you know, I'm not really comfortable being on camera.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Are you kidding me? This is fantastic! I'm a natural in front of the lens!

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Roland, you're not even in frame.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Not yet. Just wait.

jokes.text: Well, I happened to be chatting with our dear little Twyla about her party tonight, and she mentioned that you have yet to RSVP? And I thought, that's not the Jocelyn I know. What must've felled her?
jokes.setup: Moira checking on why Jocelyn hasn't RSVPed
jokes.punchline: Dramatic language like Jocelyn has been struck by tragedy
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira overdramatization of mundane social situation

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Jocelyn, darling, I haven't received your RSVP. Has something dreadful happened? Have you been struck by tragedy? Is it a death in the family? A debilitating illness? A social scandal of such magnitude that you can no longer show your face in polite society?

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: No, I just... I didn't see the invitation.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, that IS a tragedy.

jokes.text: Last year it went until 3 am, And Twyla just wandered around kind of winking at everybody, until she realized she hadn't picked a murderer.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn explaining why the parties aren't great
jokes.punchline: Twyla forgot to assign the murderer role
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific and relatable party disaster that perfectly captures small-town awkwardness

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: Twyla forgot to assign the murderer role

jokes.text: And then the year before there were three murderers, and one of them actually robbed the cafe!
jokes.setup: Jocelyn continuing party horror stories
jokes.punchline: Murder mystery party led to actual crime
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from social awkwardness to actual crime, perfectly absurd

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: So then we organized this murder mystery party, right? And someone actually stole the silverware.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: I mean, we were all so into character, nobody realized it was a real crime until the next morning.

jokes.text: This party has real momentum now. And with you attached, it's no less than a snowball gathering speed, picking up bodies as it rolls downhill. And that's good? Very good.
jokes.setup: Moira using elaborate metaphor to describe party momentum
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn's confusion about whether 'picking up bodies' sounds positive
jokes.explanation: Good Moira moment with unintentionally morbid metaphor for a murder mystery party

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: The party has really picked up bodies.

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: That sounds... positive?

jokes.text: Okay, it's just that I misplaced my high school diploma when we lost our house. No, I wish it was a flood.
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining losing her diploma
jokes.punchline: Wishing their financial ruin was a natural disaster instead
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alexis's perspective on their family's downfall

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I lost my diploma.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You lost your diploma?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I don't know where it is. It was probably in one of my bags that I left in the Hamptons, or it could be in a storage unit somewhere. I really don't know.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, this is just adding insult to injury.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: You know, I wish that our financial ruin was a natural disaster. At least then we'd have something to blame other than ourselves.

jokes.text: That's almost word for word what they just said to me on the phone.
jokes.setup: David saying colleges won't reject her for missing diploma
jokes.punchline: Alexis revealing they literally just did exactly that
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and deadpan delivery deflating David's confident assumption

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, I'm sure they won't reject you just for a missing diploma.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: They literally just did.

jokes.text: Okay, what are you doing? Reorganizing my knits!
jokes.setup: Alexis asking what David is doing
jokes.punchline: David's defensive over-explanation of mundane task
jokes.explanation: Shows David's defensiveness about his unemployed state through fashion obsession

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: What are you doing?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm organizing the store inventory system using a color-coded filing method that accounts for seasonal fluctuations, supplier lead times, and customer preference patterns. It's not just filing, Alexis. It's a comprehensive logistics solution.

jokes.text: Uh, we were playing at um... it was what course, Stevie? It was down the road. Uh, Maple... Grove? Grove, Maple Grove.
jokes.setup: Golf club asking where they played earlier
jokes.punchline: Johnny and Stevie obviously making up course name on the spot
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing their desperate attempt to seem legitimate

dialogue.speaker: Golf Club Employee
dialogue.line: Where did you play this morning?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Uh... Pebble Beach.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: No, we played... Shadow Creek.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Yes, Shadow Creek at Pebble Beach.

jokes.text: Uh, we were playing at um... it was what course, Stevie? It was down the road. Uh, Maple... Grove? Grove, Maple Grove.
jokes.setup: Golf club managers ask where they played earlier
jokes.punchline: Johnny and Stevie awkwardly improvising a fake golf course name
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy as they dig themselves deeper into a lie.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: We played at, uh... Pebble Beach.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: No, we said Torrey Pines.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Right, yes, Torrey Pines. Very exclusive.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: In... Canada.

jokes.text: Maple Grove? No, I don't know that one. Oh, really? It's a beautiful course. Very exclusive, Uh, but it is set... way back off the road, it's... Which might explain why you've never seen or heard of it.
jokes.setup: Manager doesn't know their made-up course
jokes.punchline: Johnny desperately explaining why exclusive course is unknown
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation of the lie with Johnny's increasing desperation

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: It's a very exclusive course. You wouldn't have heard of it.

dialogue.speaker: Golf Manager
dialogue.line: Where is it?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: It's in... the Hamptons.

dialogue.speaker: Golf Manager
dialogue.line: Which Hamptons course?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: It's very private. They don't advertise. Very selective membership.

jokes.text: Very exclusive, Uh, but it is set... way back off the road, it's... Which might explain why you've never seen or heard of it.
jokes.setup: The manager doesn't know the fake golf course
jokes.punchline: Johnny desperately explaining away its non-existence
jokes.explanation: Johnny digging deeper into the lie with increasingly absurd justifications.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: It's a very exclusive course. Extremely exclusive. You wouldn't know about it because... it's not open to the public. It's private. Very private.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: The fairways are maintained to the highest standard. Immaculate, really. Championship-level maintenance.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: It's just... it's not widely advertised because they prefer to keep membership limited. Word of mouth only. Very discreet operation.

jokes.text: Pub nights, frisbee in the quad... Late night hacky sack. Mhmm. We got some serious sack circles going. Oh, so many sex circles. Sack. Hacky sack.
jokes.setup: Ted nostalgic about college activities
jokes.punchline: Alexis mishearing 'sack' as 'sex' circles
jokes.explanation: Simple misheard wordplay that fits Alexis's character but isn't particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: I was just thinking about all those crazy things we used to do in college. Like throwing the sack around.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Wait, you threw sex around in college?

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: No, the sack. The football sack.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, that is so much less interesting.

jokes.text: Didn't you say on your resume that you did four years at UCLA? Yes... technically, if you consider Bel Air an extension of the campus, which everybody does.
jokes.setup: Ted questioning Alexis's college claims
jokes.punchline: Alexis's ridiculous rationalization for lying about UCLA
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis logic - technically true but completely ridiculous rationalization showing her privileged disconnect

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Wait, you didn't actually go to UCLA?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, I mean, I was *accepted*. I just didn't actually attend. But that's basically the same thing.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: That is not the same thing at all.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Okay, but I'm very knowledgeable about Los Angeles. I could have gone. I just chose not to, which I think is even more impressive.

jokes.text: But I did meet Beyonce in Mykonos, so it was almost worth it.
jokes.setup: Alexis admits to taking a semester off
jokes.punchline: Her priorities valuing celebrity encounters over education
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis logic - celebrity meetings trump education.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I took a semester off.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You took a semester off? For what?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, I was going to go back, but then I ran into Kourtney Kardashian at Nobu, and I just felt like, you know, why sit in a lecture hall when you could be networking with celebrities?

jokes.text: No, no, me and some friends, we just got in a car, and took off. Yeah, we were following the American Idol tour. We missed a couple shows, but it's always the exact same set, so... no regrets.
jokes.setup: Ted admitting he didn't finish high school on time either
jokes.punchline: Followed American Idol tour instead of finishing school
jokes.explanation: Unexpected character revelation showing Ted's own past irresponsibility

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: I followed the American Idol tour instead of finishing school.

jokes.text: Okay, let's get our golf sticks.
jokes.setup: Stevie and Johnny about to golf
jokes.punchline: Stevie calls golf clubs 'golf sticks'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie - showing her complete unfamiliarity with golf through wrong terminology

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I'm ready to go hit some golf sticks.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: They're called golf clubs.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: That's what I said. Golf sticks.

jokes.text: Did you know that according to IMDB, Rosa Parks was in an episode of "Touched By an Angel?"
jokes.setup: David in his room doing nothing
jokes.punchline: Random, pointless trivia as evidence of his boredom
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of David's internet rabbit holes and random obsessions.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Did you know that a group of flamingos is called a 'flamboyance'? I just learned that. I have nothing to do.

jokes.text: Did you know that according to IMDB, Rosa Parks was in an episode of "Touched By an Angel?" Exactly, David. You are bored, lethargic, and practically dripping with ennui!
jokes.setup: David sharing random trivia
jokes.punchline: Moira using this as evidence of his deteriorated state
jokes.explanation: Good character interplay - David's random knowledge vs Moira's dramatic diagnosis

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Did you know that honey never spoils? Archaeologists have found pots of honey in Egyptian tombs that are thousands of years old and still perfectly edible.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: David, that is the saddest thing I've ever heard. You've really hit rock bottom, haven't you? Next you'll be telling me facts about the mating habits of seahorses.

jokes.text: You are bored, lethargic, and practically dripping with ennui!
jokes.setup: Moira diagnosing David's condition
jokes.punchline: Her dramatic, over-the-top description using 'ennui'
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira - theatrical diagnosis of simple boredom.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: David, darling, what you're experiencing is a severe case of ennui. It's a malaise of the spirit, a profound weariness of the soul that only the truly cultured and sophisticated can properly diagnose. You're not depressed, you're not tired—you're ennui-ed. It's far more elegant, really.

jokes.text: You are squandering your social capital. A taste-maker like you should be out there, tasting things! Uh, I'm at the buffet, and there is nothing to taste!
jokes.setup: Moira lecturing David about wasting his influence
jokes.punchline: David's metaphor about limited options in small town
jokes.explanation: Nice metaphor extending Moira's taste-maker concept to David's limited small-town options

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: David, you have influence in this town. You cannot simply squander it on your various whims and complaints.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mother, I have the same influence as a Starbucks in a town with one Starbucks. It's not influence, it's lack of options.

jokes.text: Aw, say, don't be a dew-dropper! Throw some concealer under those peepers, make like a swell, and go put on the Ritz!
jokes.setup: Moira encouraging David to attend party
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top 1920s slang
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira - completely committing to theme with authentic period slang

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: David, you simply must attend this soirée. It will be the bee's knees, the cat's pajamas, the elephant's eyebrows!

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mother, the elephant's eyebrows? That's not even a real phrase.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, it is now, and it's absolutely the berries!

jokes.text: Uh, where are you?! You know who this is, and you know where I am. Did everyone show up? Yes, everybody showed up. Good, then my work is done. Uh no, your job is not done, You guilted me into coming here tonight!
jokes.setup: David calling Moira from the party
jokes.punchline: Moira considering her job done despite not showing up
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Moira's ability to rationalize avoiding commitment

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mom, where are you? You said you'd be here.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I know, but I've decided my work here is done.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Your work? You're not even here.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Exactly. I've successfully lowered everyone's expectations of me so much that my mere absence is now a gift.

jokes.text: They'll never catch me.
jokes.setup: David realizes Moira is the missing murderer
jokes.punchline: Moira playing along as if she's actually escaped
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira line - taking the game seriously while being completely unhelpful.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mom, you're the murderer everyone's been looking for?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I didn't *plan* to be. But when life gives you an opportunity to disappear, you simply must take it. Besides, the lighting in that abandoned warehouse was absolutely dreadful. I'm doing them a favor by staying missing.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You can't just escape from a murder investigation!

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Darling, I've escaped far worse. Three marriages, the nineties, and that unfortunate incident with the community theater production of Cats. This is nothing.

jokes.text: You should thank me. Twyla was set to throw a fiasco until I exerted my influence. Uh yeah, and the way Twyla keeps asking about where you are, I'm pretty sure you're the murderer.
jokes.setup: Moira taking credit for party success
jokes.punchline: David realizing Moira is probably the assigned murderer
jokes.explanation: Great payoff connecting Moira's absence to the party structure itself

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, the party was a tremendous success. The ambiance, the flow, the way I orchestrated every single detail.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mom, you didn't do anything. You showed up twenty minutes late.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Yes, but my presence elevated the entire evening. I was the life of the party.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You spent the whole time telling people about your colonoscopy.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, nobody else was talking about theirs.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh my God. Wait. Were you the murderer in the murder mystery game?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: What? No. Well, yes, but—

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You were assigned the murderer and you've been taking credit for the party this whole time. Of course you were.

jokes.text: Wait a minute, we were at the graduation! Yeah! I know, but I wasn't. We saw you there! Did we, though? Yeah. We were there, we might not have stayed through the "R's," I think we left halfway through the "L's."
jokes.setup: Parents insisting they saw Alexis graduate
jokes.punchline: Revealing they left the ceremony early, missing the reveal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation revealing how the parents' own behavior enabled the deception

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: We were there for your graduation, sweetheart. We saw the whole thing.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Yes, we arrived early, got excellent seats, and we were very moved.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You left after fifteen minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: That's not... we didn't leave early.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: There was an issue with the acoustics in the gymnasium.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You missed my entire name being called. You missed me walking across the stage. You missed everything.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, we got the gist of it.

jokes.text: we might not have stayed through the "R's," I think we left halfway through the "L's." Oh, yeah, it got very repetitive.
jokes.setup: They try to recall seeing Alexis at graduation
jokes.punchline: Their casual admission to leaving early because it was boring
jokes.explanation: Perfect Rose parent behavior - treating their daughter's graduation as tedious entertainment.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: We didn't actually stay for the whole thing.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: It was rather tedious.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: We left after the first half hour.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: The speeches were interminable.

jokes.text: Then how do you explain this graduation photo? Huh? That's Jess Chang! What? Jess Chang? Well, who can tell with all this hair in front of your face? That's not my face!
jokes.setup: Johnny presenting photo evidence of graduation
jokes.punchline: Photo is actually of wrong person entirely
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and payoff - the supposed 'evidence' completely falls apart in absurd way

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Look at this beautiful photo from Alexis's graduation.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Johnny, that's not Alexis.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: What do you mean? Of course it is.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Dad, I've never seen that person in my life.

tmdb_overview: Johnny and Stevie pitch a nearby golf course on a package deal with the motel. Moira tries to get out of an invitation to Twyla’s murder mystery party.
insight: Murder mystery setup lets character quirks escalate naturally across fifty jokes.
